As a result, a father and his 9-year-old daughter were killed on the spot while a mother and her 17-year-old son received gunshot wounds. the Kharkiv Region Prosecutor's Office identified the Russian soldier and tried him in absentia 'for violating the laws and customs of war, combined with premeditated murder'.
Displacement: On 17 March 2022, about 2000 civilians were evacuated from Mariupol, Donetsk to Zaporizhia after being under siege for over a week.

On 18 March 2022, Donetsk rebels' leader, joined by over a dozen United Russia MPs and two members of the Russian Federation Council, protested in Granitne, Donetsk, in support of the annexation of Crimea and in support of the ongoing war in Ukraine.

notes: Around 15 April 2022 (month of), the Russian Federal Security Services stopped a civilian car with a local resident in Vovchansk, Kharkiv. They accused him of collaborating with the Ukrainian military and drove him to the local prosecutor's office, where the Russians had set up a torture chamber. They forcibly dragged the man into the basement, where, in addition to him, there were already more than 10 illegally detained people. They tied wires to his earlobes and electrocuted him with an electric shock device, and also filed his teeth with a file. After a while, the civilian man managed to escape by swimming across the river and reached the territory controlled by Ukraine.

On 24 April 2022, during the intense siege of Mariupol, Russian forces heavily shelled Mariupol city, Donetsk. A family of three attempted to take cover but the fallen debris killed a 14-year-old girl (she was a weightlifter athlete) and her mother instantly while her son who ran after them was shot dead by a Russian sniper.

On 3 May 2022, relatives and friends of the servicemen that stayed in Azovstal plant gathered in Zaporizhia, calling on world leaders to help evacuate them from the Azovstal plant, which was besieged by Russian troops.

On 21 September 2022, a prisoner exchange took place between Russia and Ukraine (coded to Kyiv, Ukraine). 215 prisoners we returned to Ukraine, including the commanders of Azov Regiment and marines, who were held in the Russian captivity after the siege of Mariupol, as well as Mariupol police officers, paramedics etc., as well as 10 foreign citizens. 55 Russian POWs and a former pro-Russian politician Viktor Medvedchuk were transferred to Russia.
